meta_title: For Founders: SaaS pricing to Solve can't finish projects (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't finish projects. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS Pricing to Solve Project Stagnation
content.seo_description: Stop the cycle of unfinished projects. Learn how to implement strategic SaaS pricing using Antigravity and Claude Code to ensure market validation and growth.
content.answer_block: Project stagnation stems from a lack of market feedback. By implementing a 'pricing-first' architecture, you force business validation before building. Use Antigravity to scaffold your billing infrastructure and Claude Code to iterate on usage-based models, ensuring your product solves a high-value problem that users are willing to pay for immediately.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS is littered with 'perfectly engineered' products that never saw a single transaction. You find yourself stuck in a perpetual loop of refactoring and feature-creep because you are building for an abstract user rather than a paying customer. When the finish line stays perpetually out of reach, it isn't a coding problem—it’s a lack of economic gravity.

True velocity comes from the tension of a paid commitment. By integrating your pricing model into the core architecture of your MVP, you transform your project from a hobbyist experiment into a commercial machine. Stop coding in a vacuum; start building against a revenue-generating constraint that mandates completion.

steps.title: Define the Value-Based Pricing Hook
steps.description: Before writing logic, define the specific metric that drives your revenue. If your pricing isn't tied to value, your code will lack focus. This step forces you to build only what users pay for.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project requirements and propose a value-based pricing model that limits feature creep by gating high-complexity modules behind a 'Pro' tier. Focus on usage-based billing.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Generate a pricing strategy document that maps core features to specific user tiers for my SaaS MVP.'

steps.title: Scaffold the Billing Infrastructure
steps.description: Integrate Stripe or LemonSqueezy at the foundational level. By setting up the billing webhook architecture now, you ensure the project is 'transaction-ready' from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular billing controller that handles subscription states and usage-based webhooks. Use a clean, extendable pattern for future tier additions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Implement the billing controller structure and test the webhook endpoint for subscription events.'

steps.title: Implement Feature Gating Middleware
steps.description: Create a middleware layer that checks the user's subscription status before executing high-compute functions. This prevents free-tier abuse and enforces your pricing strategy in code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust middleware function that intercepts API requests and verifies user subscription status against the database. Reject non-paying users for premium routes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /edit 'Add a subscription middleware to the API router that restricts access to premium endpoints.'

steps.title: Automate Usage-Based Metering
steps.description: Track the specific metric your users pay for. If you aren't measuring it, you can't bill for it. Keep the implementation lightweight to maintain speed-to-market.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an efficient database schema and tracking service for usage-based metrics. Ensure it handles high-concurrency writes without slowing down the core app.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Create the database migrations and usage-tracking service for the subscription billing model.'

steps.title: Deploy with a 'Pay-to-Test' Constraint
steps.description: Force yourself to ship by setting a deadline where the public beta requires a paid subscription for advanced features. This final push eliminates 'perfect' in favor of 'profitable'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a deployment script that flags the production environment to strictly enforce subscription checks, effectively launching the MVP for early-access paying users.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /bash 'Deploy the application to staging and run a sanity check on the subscription enforcement logic.'

pro_tips: Always price your MVP higher than you think; it's easier to offer a discount later than to raise prices on early adopters.
Use 'Feature Flagging' to toggle premium features instantly without re-deploying your entire codebase.
Implement a 'fail-soft' mechanism where users get a grace period on billing failures to reduce immediate churn.
Prioritize building the 'billing dashboard' as one of the first 3 screens; it grounds the project in reality.
Never build an administrative panel for your SaaS until you have at least 5 paying users; focus only on the user-facing value.
content.next_steps: Once your billing logic is integrated, shift your focus to high-intent traffic acquisition. Do not add new features until you have processed your first five payments.
content.conversion_block: Stop building features that nobody wants to pay for. Align your code with your revenue model and ship your MVP with absolute confidence. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
